{{count}} sandboxed workloads are hidden by the active filters._other
A {{kind}} named \"{{name}}\" already ex: A {{kind}} named "{{name}}" already exists in {{namespace}}. Pick a different name.
Allow the cluster to reach pod VMs on Co: Allow the cluster to reach pod VMs on Compute Engine: open TCP 15150 (agent) and UDP 9000 (VXLAN tunnel) from your worker nodes. The command below is filled in from your cluster — no placeholders to edit.
Apply in cluster asks the Cloud Credenti: Apply in cluster asks the Cloud Credential Operator to mint a credential scoped to compute.firewalls.*, then runs gcloud in a Job — no local CLI or cloud console needed.
Available once the KataConfig install co: Available once the KataConfig install completes and kata-remote is registered.
Avoid deploying workloads in the Operato: Avoid deploying workloads in the Operator namespace. Create or pick a dedicated namespace instead.
Comma-separated, no spaces. Instance typ: Comma-separated, no spaces. Instance types a workload may request by annotation; leave empty to allow only the default above.
Configure the peer pods config map first: Configure the peer pods config map first. The operator reads peer-pods-cm while installing KataConfig — creating KataConfig before it means recreating KataConfig later.
Could not apply the firewall rule: Could not apply the firewall rule
Could not read worker node IPs. Replace: Could not read worker node IPs. Replace <worker_subnet_cidr> with your worker subnet (for example 10.0.128.0/24) before running the command.
Create a pull secret for peer pods: Create a pull secret for peer pods
Creating a KataConfig reboots your worke: Creating a KataConfig reboots your worker nodes
Default instance type used when a worklo: Default instance type used when a workload does not request one.
Delete {{kind}} {{name}} in {{namespace}: Delete {{kind}} {{name}} in {{namespace}}? Its sandbox VM will be torn down.
Deploy a workload with runtimeClassName:: Deploy a workload with runtimeClassName: kata-remote to run it in a pod VM.
Each node drains and reboots to install: Each node drains and reboots to install the runtime — expect roughly 5–20 min for {{nodes}} worker(s). The step turns green once the runtime is ready.
Each node drains and reboots to install the runtime — expect roughly 5–20 min per worker. The step turns green once the runtime is ready.
Editing the manifest directly — form cha: Editing the manifest directly — form changes won’t apply until you reset.
Existing pods still use the kata-remote: Existing pods still use the kata-remote runtime class, which blocks deleting this KataConfig. Delete those workloads first, then retry.
Fetch subnet, VPC, and security group ID: Fetch subnet, VPC, and security group IDs with the AWS CLI
Firewall rule applied — open the Job abo: Firewall rule applied — open the Job above to see the result.
For probes, volumes, or anything else, e: For probes, volumes, or anything else, edit the manifest directly on the Review step.
Install the kata runtime on your workers: Install the kata runtime on your workers by creating a KataConfig with peer pods enabled. Each node reboots to install it, so this takes a few minutes.
Installed — the KataConfig CRD is presen: Installed — the KataConfig CRD is present on this cluster.
Installing the Kata runtime drains and r: Installing the Kata runtime drains and reboots each eligible node — this can take from 10 to 60+ minutes. Track progress on the Sandboxes overview.
KataConfig is installed — the operator i: KataConfig is installed — the operator is registering the pod VM image. It appears here automatically once ready.
KataConfig not found — OSC is not instal: KataConfig not found — OSC is not installed
Keep this on for sandboxed containers. C: Keep this on for sandboxed containers. Confidential Containers sets DISABLECVM to false to boot pod VMs inside a TEE.
Leave blank — the operator registers an: Leave blank — the operator registers an AMI from your cluster credentials after KataConfig runs. Set this only to pin your own AMI.
Leave empty to use the cluster default:: Leave empty to use the cluster default: {{mt}}
More info for image pull secret: More info for image pull secret
Must be one of the instance types allowe: Must be one of the instance types allowed in peer-pods-cm (PODVM_INSTANCE_TYPES).
Must consist of lowercase letters, numbe: Must consist of lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, start and end with an alphanumeric character, and be at most 63 characters.
Name: Name
Name of a pull secret in this namespace,: Name of a pull secret in this namespace, for private registry images.
Namespace \"{{namespace}}\" does not exi: Namespace "{{namespace}}" does not exist. Pick an existing namespace.
Namespace \"{{namespace}}\" is terminati: Namespace "{{namespace}}" is terminating and cannot accept new workloads.
Need anything else? Edit the manifest di: Need anything else? Edit the manifest directly on the Review step.
No action needed — the operator builds a: No action needed — the operator builds and registers the pod VM image automatically when KataConfig finishes installing.
No kata runtime classes were found. Chec: No kata runtime classes were found. Check that the KataConfig has finished installing on the Sandboxes overview page.
No pods found for this deployment.
No results match the current filters: No results match the current filters
Nodes reboot during install, so this can: Nodes reboot during install, so this can take several minutes depending on how many workers you have. The step turns green once the runtime is ready.
Note: peer pods run in a remote VM, so n: Note: peer pods run in a remote VM, so node-side metrics may not reflect in-VM usage.
On-node Kata boots the microVM directly: On-node Kata boots the microVM directly on the worker node, so those nodes must expose hardware virtualization (KVM): native on bare-metal workers, or nested virtualization on VM-based workers. Make sure your nodes have it — in a mixed cluster, on-node pods only schedule onto nodes that do. If you are not sure, a peer-pods runtime class runs each pod in its own cloud VM and needs neither.
One KEY=value per line, added alongside: One KEY=value per line, added alongside app={{name}}.
One KEY=value per line. Non-identifying: One KEY=value per line. Non-identifying metadata for tools and pipelines.
One KEY=value per line. The pod schedule: One KEY=value per line. The pod schedules only onto matching nodes.
Open the peer pods communication ports (: Open the peer pods communication ports (15150 and 9000) so your worker nodes can reach the pod VMs. The command below is filled in from your cluster — run it in your cloud CLI.
Open the peer pods communication ports —: Open the peer pods communication ports — TCP 15150 (agent) and UDP 9000 (VXLAN tunnel) — in your cloud firewall, allowing traffic from your worker nodes to the pod VMs. This is done in your cloud CLI, not from the cluster.

Open the peer pods communication ports — TCP 15150 (agent) and UDP 9000 (VXLAN tunnel) — so your worker nodes can reach the pod VMs. The command below is filled in from your cluster; run it in your cloud CLI.
Optional kataConfigPoolSelector. Leave e: Optional kataConfigPoolSelector. Leave empty to install kata-remote on all worker nodes.
Overlay (VXLAN) UDP port between worker: Overlay (VXLAN) UDP port between worker and pod VM (default 9000).
Override the default pod VM image with a: Override the default pod VM image with a custom image ID (AMI or Azure image) compatible with your cloud. Leave blank to use the image the operator registered.
Peer pods pull the image inside the pod: Peer pods pull the image inside the pod VM, so the secret must exist in this namespace. Copy the cluster pull secret in (and optionally link it to the default service account):
PEERPODS_LIMIT_PER_NODE — maximum pod VM: PEERPODS_LIMIT_PER_NODE — maximum pod VMs per worker (default 10).
Prefilled from your cluster where availa: Prefilled from your cluster where available. Pod VM AMI ID is added automatically by the operator after KataConfig runs — you don’t set it here (pin a custom AMI under Advanced options if needed).
Reach pod VMs over their public IP (only: Reach pod VMs over their public IP (only if they are on a different VPC)
Registered automatically by the operator: Registered automatically by the operator — peer pods boot from this image. No manual build needed.
Replace the placeholder value(s) before: Replace the placeholder value(s) before running: {{placeholders}}. Find them in your peer-pods config map or cloud console.
Required on clouds without nested virtua: Required on clouds without nested virtualization (e.g. most GCP/AWS/Azure). Create the peer-pods-cm before this KataConfig — the operator builds and registers the pod VM image itself once it installs.
ROOT_VOLUME_SIZE — pod VM root disk in G: ROOT_VOLUME_SIZE — pod VM root disk in GB (default and minimum 6).
Run pods as cloud VMs (kata-remote) via: Run pods as cloud VMs (kata-remote) via the cloud-api-adaptor
Run the container as a non-root user: Run the container as a non-root user
Sandboxed workloads run inside a dedicat: Sandboxed workloads run inside a dedicated virtual machine, isolating them from the host kernel and from other workloads. Pick the isolation level that fits:
Sandboxed workloads run inside a dedicated VM for kernel-level isolation. Create one with a kata runtime class to see it here.
Set the cloud provider, networking, and: Set the cloud provider, networking, and pod VM sizing the cloud-api-adaptor uses.
Set the number of replicas for this depl: Set the number of replicas for this deployment.
Taking too long? Troubleshoot the image: Taking too long? Troubleshoot the image build
The Job failed — open it above to read t: The Job failed — open it above to read the logs. The minted credential may still be provisioning, or the rule may already exist with conflicting settings.
The manifest needs a kind and a metadata: The manifest needs a kind and a metadata.name.
The operator builds the image in a Job. If it has not appeared after a few minutes, check the Job events and logs:
The operator picks the smallest instance: The operator picks the smallest instance type that meets this vCPU and memory floor.
The operator reconciles the cloud-api-ad: The operator reconciles the cloud-api-adaptor DaemonSet from this ConfigMap. On Google Cloud, credentials are minted automatically by the Cloud Credential Operator — no peer-pods-secret is needed unless you removed the CCO. The operator builds and registers the pod VM image automatically when KataConfig installs — no separate image config needed.
Tick this once you have opened the firew: Tick this once you have opened the firewall ports in your cloud — it turns the step green. The GCP "Apply in cluster" action ticks it for you.
Timed out waiting for the Cloud Credenti: Timed out waiting for the Cloud Credential Operator to mint a credential. Your cluster may not be in mint mode — run the command above with your own credentials instead.
Waiting for the kata runtime to finish i: Waiting for the kata runtime to finish installing before workloads can run.
Your workers have no external IP, so tra: Your workers have no external IP, so traffic reaches the pod VMs via the cluster’s Cloud NAT address. Replace <cluster_nat_egress_ip> with that IP before running the command.
